sourceDesc.p: Ernest A. Wallis Budge, The Book of Paradise, Being the Histories and Sayings of the Monks and Ascetics of the Egyptian Desert by Palladius, Hieronymus and Others: The Syriac Texts, According to the Recension of ʿAnân-Îshôʿ of Bêth ʿÂbhê, Edited with an English Translation, vol. 1, Lady Meux Manuscript 6 (London: W. Drugulin, 1904), p. 181.

Transcription of the text downloaded from Wikisource on 10/27/2024: https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/The_Paradise/Volume_1/Book_1/The_Paradise_of_Palladius/The_Histories_of_the_Holy_Men/History_57 . This Wikisource transcription is available under a Creative Commons Attribution-ShareAlike License

body.head: Of the Blessed Man Kapiton
body.ab: Now by the side of this man there used to dwell Kapiton, a man who had been formerly a thief, and he had lived in holes in the rocks for fifty years, [at a place] which was about four miles from Antinoë; he never went down from his cave, not even to the river Nile, for he used to say that he was unable to meet in the congregation of the children of men him that still fought with him, namely, his Adversary.
